quotes.topic: Finding chord voicings with melody on top
quotes.verbatim_quote:
The next step is to find chord voicings that have the melody notes
on top.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This states the fundamental goal of the harmonization process—melody-driven voicing selection is the core principle for combining melody and harmony on guitar.

quotes.verbatim_quote: It is often necessary to raise the melody one octave in order to use larger chord voicings.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This teaches a practical technique for expanding harmonic possibilities when a melody note would otherwise be too low for effective chord voicing.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When using a lead-sheet (a sketch of a song that shows words, melody and chord
symbols only) as a source for your basic melody and chord changes, it is generally
helpful to raise the melody an octave. Check the highest and lowest notes in the
song to see if an octave change would make the tune easier to harmonize.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule establishes a standard practice for adapting lead-sheet melodies to guitar, providing a decision framework for octave placement.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Consider the possibility that the song might lie on the fingerboard better in a

different key. Changing the key can often change the basic character of the tune
itself. For instance, you may want to try keys that allow the use of open strings.
The extra investigation is usually well worth the effort.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule teaches that key choice impacts both playability and musical character, directing arrangers to explore multiple keys beyond the original.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Learn to play the melody in single notes first. Explore it in every octave to make

sure you understand what the melody is about.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This foundational step establishes that deep melody understanding must precede harmonic arrangement and shapes the entire approach to chord melody.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Memorize chord changes and be able to play them in various areas of the finger-

board.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule emphasizes the importance of fingerboard knowledge and flexibility in executing harmony options across multiple positions.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Arrange your song with the basic chords first. When you have a handle on the basic

arrangement, then dress up the harmonies with passing chords, substitutions
and harmonizations.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This procedural rule establishes the correct sequencing: build structural foundation before adding decorative harmonic techniques.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Don't carve your arrangements or chord changes in stone. Basically, you want

skeletal arrangements from which you can improvise melodically, harmonically
and rhythmically. Once you gain confidence with this approach, it is a lot more
fun, which, never forget, is the whole idea.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule reframes arrangement as a flexible, improvisational tool rather than a fixed composition, emphasizing musical creativity and enjoyment.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
To
enhance means to simply add extensions or alterations to the basic chord. To most jazz
musicians, all the chords within a given quality, or family, (major, minor, dominant, etc.) are
interchangeable. In other words, a Major 6th chord can be thought of in the same light as a
Major 9th chord or a Major 7th chord. Functionally, they are all the same.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This defines the conceptual framework for understanding chord enhancement—chords within the same family are functionally equivalent, enabling flexible substitution.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
In the chart below, all the chords are interchangeable with any other chord in the same
column.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This establishes a practical decision rule: within a chord family column, any voicing is acceptable, streamlining the arrangement process.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
It should be noted that chord enhancement is not the same as chord substitution. Chord
substitution implies replacing a chord with another chord that has a different root.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This distinction clarifies two different harmonic techniques and prevents confusion between adding extensions and changing harmonic function.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Context is everything. Taste should be
your primary concern. Not everything needs to be enriched.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule provides a quality standard that tempers the previous explanation of enhancement: musical judgment should guide when and how much to enhance.
